title: phyllocladus protractus
intro: Phyllocladus protractus, also known as the pukatea, is a species of coniferous tree native to New Zealand, characterized by its distinctive leaves that resemble scales. The plant has been used in traditional Maori medicine for various purposes, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The medicinal properties of Phyllocladus protractus are said to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made into a decoction or infusion for consumption, often used to treat various ailments and maintain overall well-being.
